before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"de60m","text":"Using the Excel Reagent Calculator","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8r13s","text":"For efficient and accurate reagent preparation, please use the attached Excel file","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ar0p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6qlua","text":"Open the file and locate the blue box labeled \"Number of samples?\".","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13},{"style":"bold","offset":29,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1fq80","text":"Enter the number of samples you are processing.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bv5f3","text":"The spreadsheet will automatically calculate the total volumes needed for each master mix in the corresponding green boxes. These calculations include a surplus for one extra reaction (n+0.5) to prevent shortages due to pipetting error.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":111,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f0f1","text":"Use the volumes from the green boxes to prepare your master mixes.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":25,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3j66f","text":"The purple boxes indicate the volume of master mix to add to each individual sample.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":4,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"25n23","text":"High-Efficiency Double-Stranded cDNA Synthesis from dsRNA Templates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":67}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fpver","text":"A cost-effective, custom protocol for preparing viral dsRNA for next-generation sequencing using SuperScript IV.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"83ubb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6nka8","text":"This protocol details a robust and cost-effective method for synthesizing double-stranded cDNA (ds-cDNA) from dsRNA templates. It is optimized for low-input dsRNA, such as that extracted from complex environmental samples (e.g., soil viromes). The protocol uses a two-step process, starting with first-strand synthesis using SuperScript IV Reverse Transcriptase, followed by second-strand synthesis using Klenow Polymerase. The resulting ds-cDNA is suitable for library preparation and next-generation sequencing. This method serves as a reliable alternative to commercial kits.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2pmr1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bckbs","text":"This protocol is a key downstream component of our SS-VIME (Single-Source Virome-Microbiome Extraction) workflow, recently accepted for publication in Microbiology Spectrum. After using the primary SS-VIME protocol to co-extract genomic DNA and dsRNA from a single environmental sample, this method is specifically designed to process the purified dsRNA fraction, enabling the characterization of the active RNA virome. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":51,"length":8},{"style":"bold","offset":150,"length":22},{"style":"italic","offset":151,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":198,"length":16}],"data":{}},{"key":"od37","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eo1kb","text":"To facilitate high-throughput use, an Excel-based reagent calculator is provided in the \"Attachments\" section. Users simply input the number of samples, and the spreadsheet automatically calculates the required master mix volumes, including a built-in surplus to account for pipetting inaccuracies.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":38,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"89hb6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a1r7","text":"Background","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13puo","text":"While many commercial kits are available, they can be costly, especially for large-scale projects. This protocol was developed and optimized to provide a high-yield, cost-effective, and reliable workflow for generating high-quality ds-cDNA. It has been successfully used in our lab to prepare sequencing libraries from dsRNA isolated from challenging soil matrices, enabling the study of the active soil virome.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8jpsr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4n98h","text":"\nNote: The associated manuscript, titled \"SS-VIME: A Single-Source Virome-Microbiome Extraction Protocol Toward Comprehensive Soil Community Analysis,\" is currently in press at Microbiology Spectrum. Until the official manuscript DOI is available, please cite this protocol using its protocols.io DOI.","type":"align-center","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":1,"length":300},{"style":"bold","offset":41,"length":110}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":297,"length":3}],"data":{}},{"key":"8cp39","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"6FDD5F28D3FB4BD7803DE57ADEECE236","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kxygxp64zl8j/v1"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"26AB26586D70411CB33BB333775C660D","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5qpvobdybl4o/v1"}}}}

status.info: We use this protocol and it's working

steps.step: {"blocks":[{"key":"cd6bb","text":"Thaw all reagents on ice. Briefly centrifuge all tubes before opening to collect contents at the bottom.\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ec1hg","text":"Keep enzymes on ice or in a cold block at all times.\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8i7d5","text":"When preparing master mixes, calculate the required volume for your number of samples (n) plus at least one extra reaction to account for pipetting error (i.e., calculate for n+0.5). This is reflected in the Excel calculator’s “err.” column.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"4kl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e44pd","text":"Step 1: Prepare Denaturation Mix ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7iluc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9rthh","text":"In a nuclease-free tube, prepare the following master mix. The volumes below are for a single reaction. Multiply by n+0.5 for your master mix.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":116,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3d9qh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4adtr","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2v5qo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","B_5":{"className":"_dt-bold"}},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":["196","177"],"data":[["Component","Volume per reaction (µL)"],["Random hexamers (50 µM)","1"],["dNTPs (10 mM)","1"],["Nuclease-free water","1"],["Add to each sample (µL)","3"]],"guid":"2B96334C30AD43E58B085623B752B071","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["Component","Volume per reaction (µL)"],["Random hexamers (50 µM)","1"],["dNTPs (10 mM)","1"],["Nuclease-free water","1"],["Add to each sample (µL)","3"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23]}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"4ec3c","text":"Step 2: Set up Denaturation Reaction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":36}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dne90","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"89bkm","text":"In individual nuclease-free PCR tubes, add 10 µL of your dsRNA template.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":43,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hksa","text":"Add 3 µL of First-Strand Master Mix #1 to each tube. The total volume should be 13 µL.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":4,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eum46","text":"Gently mix by pipetting and centrifuge briefly.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"7t01c","text":"Step 3: Incubation - Denaturation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4k6ml","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c088n","text":"Place the tubes in a thermocycler and run the following program:\n    - 5 minutes at 95°C\n    - Hold at 4°C","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8d5rs","text":"Immediately after the program finishes, place the tubes on ice for at least 1 minute.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"4i6gg","text":"Step 4: Prepare First-Strand Master Mix (RT Mix)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":48}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"debv3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"caio","text":"While the denaturation is running, prepare the following master mix. The volumes below are for a single reaction. Multiply by n+0.5.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":126,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cljqt","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bgnq8","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"dn5qa","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","6_0":"","6_1":"","B_7":{"className":"_dt-bold"}},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":["240","181"],"data":[["Component","Volume per reaction (µL)"],["SuperScript IV Buffer (5X)","4"],["DTT (100 mM)","1"],["Inhibitor, Murine RNase (40 U/µL)","0.5"],["SuperScript IV RT (200 U/µL)","1"],["Nuclease-free water","0.5"],["Add to each sample (µL)","7"]],"guid":"42C1BED372E544278CC882681879C5FD","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["Component","Volume per reaction (µL)"],["SuperScript IV Buffer (5X)","4"],["DTT (100 mM)","1"],["Inhibitor, Murine RNase (40 U/µL)","0.5"],["SuperScript IV RT (200 U/µL)","1"],["Nuclease-free water","0.5"],["Add to each sample (µL)","7"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23,23]}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"4dbq1","text":"Step 5: First-Strand Synthesis","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"q0c6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8eb7q","text":"Add 7 µL of First-Strand Master Mix #2 to each of your 13 µL reactions on ice. The total volume is now 20 µL.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3sruh","text":"Mix gently by pipetting and centrifuge briefly.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"ak0mk","text":"Step 6: Incubation - First-Strand Synthesis","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6o538","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7s74g","text":"Return the tubes to the thermocycler and run the following program:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fi4ha","text":"         - 10 minutes at 23°C (for primer annealing)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4u0jl","text":"         - 30 minutes at 55°C (for reverse transcription)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5g6m4","text":"         - 10 minutes at 80°C (to inactivate the enzyme)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fm0bg","text":"         - Hold at 4°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"enceh","text":"After the program finishes, place the tubes on ice for at least 1 minute. You can proceed to the next step or store the samples at -20°C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"89bk9","text":"In a nuclease-free tube, prepare the following master mix. The volumes below are for a single reaction. Multiply by n+0.5.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4focl","text":"Pro-Tip: The RNase H volume is very small (0.2 µL). When preparing the master mix, pipette this component carefully and ensure the mix is thoroughly vortexed before aliquoting.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1lo6m","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"en6pa","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"5fa2t","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","6_0":"","6_1":"","B_7":{"className":"_dt-bold"}},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":["211","174"],"data":[["Component","Volume per reaction (µL)"],["Klenow 10X buffer (+NAD)","2.5"],["dNTP (10 mM)","1"],["Klenow polymerase (5 U/µL)","0.5"],["E.coli Ligase I (10 U/µL)","1"],["Rnase H (5 U/µL)","0.2"],["Add to each sample (µL)","5.2"]],"guid":"A4B301726DDC4A419894F5047DCD9879","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["Component","Volume per reaction (µL)"],["Klenow 10X buffer (+NAD)","2.5"],["dNTP (10 mM)","1"],["Klenow polymerase (5 U/µL)","0.5"],["E.coli Ligase I (10 U/µL)","1"],["Rnase H (5 U/µL)","0.2"],["Add to each sample (µL)","5.2"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23,23]}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"bc86m","text":"Step 8: Second-Strand Synthesis","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1qjio","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2rri1","text":"To each 20 µL first-strand reaction, add 5.2 µL of Second-Strand Master Mix #3. The final volume will be 25.2 µL.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"61erj","text":"Mix gently by pipetting and centrifuge briefly.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"2pmm2","text":"Step 9: Incubation - Second-Strand Synthesis","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":44}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m5k4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3qdod","text":"Return the tubes to the thermocycler and run the following program:

-  150 minutes (2.5 hours) at 16°C
-  20 minutes at 75°C (to inactivate enzymes)
-  Hold at 4°C","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"182mn","text":"Step 10: Bead Purification","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1gr6m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjatu","text":"Perform a standard SPRI bead cleanup to purify the final double-stranded cDNA.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4s1lm","text":"Use a bead-to-sample ratio of 1.8X. For a ~25 µL sample, this corresponds to 45 µL of beads.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":30,"length":4},{"style":"bold","offset":77,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cet1g","text":"Follow the bead manufacturer's instructions for binding, washing (with 80% ethanol), and elution.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eovj4","text":"Elute the purified ds-cDNA in an appropriate volume (e.g., 20-30 µL) of nuclease-free water or buffer. The cDNA is now ready for quantification and library preparation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
